message:
Fix beads graph node glow rendering, improve mobile responsiveness

Canvas2D glow rendering fix:
- Move shadowColor and shadowBlur setup BEFORE ctx.fill(). Previously
  these properties were set after the fill call, meaning the glow
  effect was configured too late and never actually rendered on the
  node circles. The shadow properties must be active when the draw
  call (fill) executes.
- Wrap the entire node render callback in ctx.save()/ctx.restore()
  to properly isolate canvas state between nodes. Without this, shadow
  and font settings leaked across successive nodeCanvasObject calls,
  causing unpredictable rendering artifacts.
- Explicitly clear shadowBlur to 0 before drawing text labels so
  glow does not bleed into the ID strings.
- Bump shadowBlur from 10 to 15 per globalScale for more visible glow.
- Increase label opacity from 0.7 to 0.8 and vertical offset from
  4 to 5 per globalScale for better readability.

Mobile responsiveness:
- Reduce graph container minimum height from 650px to 400px on mobile
  (min-h-[400px] md:min-h-[650px]) to prevent the graph from forcing
  excessive vertical scroll on small viewports.
- Add scale-75 md:scale-100 on the status legend bar at the bottom
  of the graph so it fits within narrow mobile screens without
  horizontal overflow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
